config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya karena alasan kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Di Indonesia, saya melihat terlalu mudah bagi "kepentingan umum" dipakai sebagai alasan untuk membungkam kritik atau memperlakukan orang secara tidak adil. Kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa meniadakan martabat dan kebebasan individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that idea makes me wary because “the public good” has often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially when unpopular people have the least power to defend themselves. Society absolutely needs limits and shared responsibilities, but those limits should be narrow and tied to real harm, not a vague appeal to the majority. If individual rights are treated as secondary, then they stop being rights and become privileges the powerful can override whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju: hak individu tidak boleh otomatis kalah oleh kepentingan masyarakat. Pengalaman di Indonesia justru menunjukkan bahwa dalih “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk menekan suara minoritas, mengabaikan prosedur, atau membenarkan ketidakadilan yang seharusnya bisa dicegah. Kepentingan sosial itu penting, tapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar dan prinsip bahwa negara maupun mayoritas tidak boleh semena-mena. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi individu sambil mengejar kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to excuse abuse by majorities and authorities. The strongest point in the other side is that society does need coordination and limits, but those limits have to be tightly connected to preventing real harm, not just serving convenience or public pressure. If we let “the common good” outrank rights by default, then rights become fragile and only exist when they are politically popular. A healthy society protects people first and then asks them to share responsibility, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, terutama ketika yang terdampak adalah kelompok kecil atau suara yang tidak populer. Memang masyarakat butuh aturan dan tanggung jawab bersama, tetapi itu justru harus dibangun di atas perlindungan hak dasar, bukan dengan mengorbankannya. Kalau hak individu bisa dikesampingkan kapan saja atas nama mayoritas, maka yang tersisa bukan kepentingan umum yang sehat, melainkan kekuasaan yang mudah menyalahgunakan legitimasi sosial.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, I have seen how appeals to the "common good" can be used to justify censorship, discrimination, and overreach, especially against unpopular minorities who have the least power to push back. Society does need rules and shared obligations, but those should be limited to preventing real harm, not used as a blank check for the majority. If rights can be overridden whenever it is socially convenient, then they are not real rights at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
